# Progressions-excerpt fetch status

*Generated by Phase 0.3b. Source: `docs/research/Progressions-K-5.pdf` (compiled Common Core Standards Writing Team, 2023).*

## Summary

| Status | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Succeeded fully** (all 4 sections populated from the PDF) | 32 of 44 | Verbatim quote + can-do + progression + representations all drawn from the Progressions text. |
| **Succeeded partially** (some sections empty because the Progressions doesn't explicitly cover them) | 12 of 44 | Most often the Progressions doesn't name *specific* misconceptions for a given sub-ID. Flagged inline in each file with the explicit note "Not explicitly named in the Progressions for [standard]." |
| **Failed entirely** (no relevant section found) | 0 of 44 | All 44 placeholder standards now have at least a verbatim quote and the can-do/progression/representations bullets drawn from the PDF. |

Plus the 5 K.OA folders (K.OA.A.1 – K.OA.A.5) that already had authentic excerpts from the in-repo Chesure file — left untouched as instructed.

Total mapping-kit folders now with real excerpts: **49 of 49**.

## Methodology notes

- The compiled PDF was extracted page-by-page to plain text using `pypdf` (see `scripts/extract_progressions.py`).
- Page-by-page text lives at `docs/research/_progressions_pages/page-NNN.txt` (332 files; ~12 MB). These are derived artifacts, not source. Safe to delete.
- All excerpts were generated by `scripts/write_progressions_excerpts.py`. Re-run that script to regenerate the files from the embedded source-of-truth strings.
- **No fabrication.** Where the Progressions does not name a misconception for a specific sub-ID, the file says so explicitly (e.g., "Not explicitly named in the Progressions for K.CC.7") rather than inventing one. Drafters who need a misconception list can pull from the Chesure knowledge files (`docs/agents/chesure-knowledge/*.md`).
- **Verbatim quoting.** All blockquoted text is reproduced from the PDF with minimal cleanup (the PDF extraction sometimes mangles math symbols and superscripts; I have substituted `×` for the corrupted multiplication sign and `?` for the box-shaped unknown marker, but otherwise the wording is unchanged).

## What's left for Phase 1+

- The five K.OA folders already had Chesure-derived excerpts and were left untouched per instructions.
- Drafters working on individual standards in Phase 1 may want to **enrich the misconception bullets** for the "Partial" rows by pulling from `docs/agents/chesure-knowledge/k-oa-progressions.md` and adjacent files. The Progressions itself is silent on most sub-ID-specific misconceptions; the Chesure files are where the project's misconception lists live.
